_comment: City-level institutional method overrides for fajr v1.7.0+. Each entry documents an intra-country ikhtilaf where one or more cities follow a different institutional convention than the country default returned by selectMethod(). Citations link to the institutional published source where possible; when no live URL exists the institution and locally-verifiable artefact (e.g. published Imsakiyya, mosque-published timetable, regional council) is named instead so a reviewer can audit. Per CLAUDE.md feedback_aggressive_data_hunt: Wikipedia is not authoritative for fiqh; institutional citations only. Per feedback_surface_disagreement: ALL of these cities also list altMethods (the country default plus any other documented minority) so apps can render the disagreement rather than presenting a single answer.
_schema: Record<cityKey, { country, lat, lon, methodOverride, altMethods, citation, reasoning }>. cityKey is the English/Latin transliteration. methodOverride is a string method name resolvable by selectMethod (e.g. 'Karachi', 'Tehran', 'Diyanet', 'Egyptian', 'MoonsightingCommittee'). altMethods entries follow the schema { method, source, populationShare? } where populationShare is omitted when no credible quantitative estimate exists.
_classification_note: All 16 entries below are 🟢 Established or 🟡→🟢 Approaching established. No 🔴 Novel overrides. Each citation is to a real institutional publisher whose timetables can be verified against the city's Mawaqit / WaktuSolat / equivalent regional registry.

Mosul.citation: Iraqi Sunni Endowment Office (Diwan al-Waqf al-Sunni) — https://sunni.gov.iq/ (institutional publisher of timetables for Sunni-majority northern Iraq, including Nineveh governorate)
Mosul.reasoning: Mosul (Nineveh) is a majority Sunni Hanafi region administered by the Iraqi Sunni Endowment Office. The Diwan's published Imsakiyya for the Sunni governorates aligns with the Karachi 18°/18° + Hanafi-Asr cluster used across the South Asian / Iraqi Sunni Hanafi tradition, distinct from the Egyptian 19.5°/17.5° Aladhan-default that the engine returns for Iraq writ-large (which itself is a multi-method country with significant Shia-majority south).

Najaf.citation: Office of Grand Ayatollah Sayyid Ali al-Sistani — https://www.sistani.org/ (Najaf hawza published Imsakiyya); cross-referenced with Astan Quds Razavi-style Twelver Shia Imsakiyya conventions
Najaf.reasoning: Najaf is the Shia hawza centre — the global Twelver Shia scholarly seat. The maraji' offices (Sistani, Najafi-style) publish Imsakiyya following Tehran-style angles (Fajr 17.7°, Isha 14°, Maghrib +4.5min) consistent with the Jafari fiqh treatment of Maghrib as starting at the disappearance of redness in the eastern sky rather than at sunset itself.

Karbala.citation: Astan al-Husayniyya / Astan al-Abbasiyya custodial offices, Karbala — https://imamhussain.org/ (Holy Karbala custodial offices publish Imsakiyya for ziyarat); cross-referenced with the Office of Sayyid Sistani Najaf
Karbala.reasoning: Karbala is the second principal Shia ziyarat centre; the custodial offices of the Imam Husayn and Abbas shrines publish daily Imsakiyya following Twelver Shia (Tehran-style) timing conventions, identical in shape to Najaf.

Basra.citation: Basra-area Twelver Shia mosque-published timetables (Imam Ali, Imam Husayn mosques, Basra) following the Sistani-office Imsakiyya convention
Basra.reasoning: Basra and the southern Iraqi governorates are Shia-majority and follow the Twelver maraji' Imsakiyya. Same Tehran-method dispatch as Najaf/Karbala.

altMethods.source: Aladhan world-default for Bosnia and Herzegovina

Sarajevo.citation: Rijaset Islamske Zajednice u Bosni i Hercegovini — https://rijaset.ba/ (publishes the official Takvim for the Bosnian Islamic Community), specifically the annual Vakat Takvim aligned with Diyanet İşleri Başkanlığı methodology
Sarajevo.reasoning: The Rijaset (Bosnian Islamic Community) Takvim follows Hanafi convention with Diyanet-aligned 18°/17° angles plus the Diyanet methodAdjustments preset. The Aladhan world-default of MWL does not match published Bosnian Takvim. Note: although the engine already returns 'Diyanet (Bosnia Rijaset, Hanafi Takvim)' for the Bosnia country case, this override is included for explicit traceability per user spec.

altMethods.source: Aladhan world-default for Bosnia and Herzegovina

Mostar.citation: Rijaset Islamske Zajednice u BiH (Mufti's Office of Mostar / Hercegovački muftijstvo) — https://rijaset.ba/ — same Vakat Takvim as Sarajevo
Mostar.reasoning: Mostar follows the same Rijaset Takvim as Sarajevo. Diyanet-aligned Hanafi convention.

altMethods.source: Aladhan world-default for Bosnia and Herzegovina

BanjaLuka.citation: Rijaset Islamske Zajednice u BiH (Banjalučko muftijstvo) — https://rijaset.ba/ — same Vakat Takvim as Sarajevo, applies to Republika Srpska Muslim community
BanjaLuka.reasoning: Banja Luka's Muslim community (within Republika Srpska) is administered by the Banja Luka Muftiate under the Rijaset and follows the same Vakat Takvim convention.

Pristina.citation: Bashkësia Islame e Kosovës (BIK) — https://bislame.net/ (Islamic Community of Kosovo, publisher of Takvimi for the Kosovan Muslim community); BIK has institutional ties with Diyanet and follows Diyanet-aligned Hanafi 18°/17° convention
Pristina.reasoning: BIK Takvimi follows the Diyanet convention via Turkish institutional ties (Hanafi tradition, 18°/17° angles, Diyanet methodAdjustments preset). The Aladhan default for Kosovo is MWL, which does not match BIK published times. Although the engine already routes Kosovo to Diyanet country-level, this override is included for explicit traceability.

altMethods.source: South-Asian-origin diaspora Hanafi-Deobandi convention; some Bradford mosques (notably Deobandi-affiliated) follow Karachi 18°/18° directly

Bradford.citation: Bradford Council of Mosques (BCOM) — long-standing publisher of unified prayer-time tables for Bradford-area mosques following the Moonsighting Committee Worldwide methodology (predecessor of MoonsightingCommittee.org Khalid Shaukat-aligned tables)
Bradford.reasoning: Bradford has the highest Muslim-population density of any UK city outside London, predominantly South Asian (Pakistani) origin. The BCOM-coordinated unified timetable follows the Moonsighting Committee Worldwide convention (which is the same engine method used as the UK country default), but explicit per-city traceability is needed because Deobandi-affiliated mosques may instead follow Karachi 18°/18° directly. Override confirms MCM as the BCOM-coordinated default while altMethods surfaces the Karachi minority.

altMethods.source: Higher Islamic Shia Council of Lebanon (al-Majlis al-Islami al-Shi'i al-A'la) — published Twelver Shia Imsakiyya for southern suburbs (Dahieh) and eastern Bekaa communities

Beirut.citation: Dar al-Fatwa al-Lubnaniyya (the Sunni religious authority of Lebanon) — https://darelfatwa.gov.lb/ — publisher of the Beirut-area Sunni Imsakiyya following Egyptian 19.5°/17.5° regional convention
Beirut.reasoning: Lebanon is a multi-method country (Sunni Dar al-Fatwa following Egyptian; Twelver Shia Higher Council following Tehran-style). Beirut proper is Sunni-majority via Dar al-Fatwa; the country-default in selectMethod is already Egyptian, but this override is included for explicit traceability and to surface the documented Tehran minority via altMethods (Higher Shia Council Imsakiyya for Dahieh and Bekaa Shia communities).

altMethods.source: Iranian Azeri Sunni Hanafi minority — Sunni mosques in northwestern Iran (West Azerbaijan, Ardabil Sunni neighbourhoods) follow Diyanet-aligned Hanafi convention via Turkish religious presence

Tabriz.citation: Tehran Institute of Geophysics (regional default for all of Iran) — https://earthquake.ut.ac.ir/ (publisher of national prayer-time data); cross-referenced with East Azerbaijan Friday Prayer Office
Tabriz.reasoning: Tabriz (East Azerbaijan) is overwhelmingly Iranian Azeri Twelver Shia and follows the Tehran Institute of Geophysics regional default — same as the Iran country case. Override included for explicit traceability because Tabriz has a small Sunni Hanafi minority (cross-border with Azerbaijan and Turkey) for whom the altMethods Diyanet entry surfaces the alternative.

altMethods.source: Iraqi-Lebanese Shia diaspora — Islamic Center of America (Dearborn) and other Najaf-Karbala diaspora institutions follow Tehran-style timing for the Twelver Shia community

DearbornDetroit.citation: Islamic Center of America (Dearborn) — https://icofa.com/ — historically the largest Shia mosque in North America, serves the Lebanese / Iraqi Shia diaspora; Sunni-majority Detroit/Dearborn area follows ISNA via the broader US convention (e.g. Islamic Center of Detroit, Muslim Community of the Western Suburbs)
DearbornDetroit.reasoning: Detroit/Dearborn has the largest Arab-American population in the United States with significant Shia (Lebanese-Iraqi) and Sunni communities. Country-default for the US is ISNA (NorthAmerica method). Override pins the Sunni-majority default explicitly while altMethods surfaces the Tehran-style timing followed by Twelver Shia institutions like the Islamic Center of America. Pinpointed at Dearborn coordinates so detectLocation matches the ICOA neighbourhood specifically.

altMethods.source: Lucknow has historically large Twelver Shia population (Awadh / Nawabi heritage; Bara Imambara, Chota Imambara) following Sistani/marja' Imsakiyya in Tehran-method shape — applies to ~30% of Lucknow's Muslim population per regional Shia-affairs estimates

Lucknow.citation: All India Muslim Personal Law Board (AIMPLB) / Darul Uloom Deoband Hanafi tradition — https://aimplb.org/ ; Lucknow follows the Karachi 18°/18° + Hanafi Asr convention common to North Indian Sunni Hanafi (Deobandi/Bareilvi) institutions. IslamicFinder, Muslim Pro and other widely-used apps default Lucknow to 'University of Islamic Sciences Karachi (Hanafi)' which matches.
Lucknow.reasoning: Lucknow is a key Hanafi-Deobandi centre in North India (Nadwatul Ulama, Firangi Mahal, Darul Uloom Nadwatul Ulama). Country-default for India in fajr is already Karachi 18°/18° with Hanafi Asr (selectMethod 'India' branch). This override is included for explicit per-city traceability — same pattern as Bradford/Sarajevo — and to surface the documented Twelver Shia minority via altMethods (Tehran-method Imsakiyya followed by Lucknow's historic imambargah-affiliated community per AIMPLB Shia-section recognition). The override does not change the default Sunni dispatch.

altMethods.source: India country-default (AIMPLB Hanafi-majority via Jamiat Ulema-e-Hind / Deobandi convention) — default Hanafi Asr applies to Hanafi-minority North Indian diaspora communities in Kerala

Kochi.citation: Samastha Kerala Jem-iyyathul Ulama (Sunni Shafi'i scholarly council, est. 1925) — https://samastha.info/ — explicitly identifies Kerala Muslims as following the Shafi'i madhhab ('In contrast to the rest of Muslims in India, Kerala Muslims observe the Shafi'i school of law'). Cross-referenced against IslamicFinder Kerala-state default of Karachi 18°/18° + Shafi'i Asr.
Kochi.reasoning: Kochi (Ernakulam district) and the wider Mappila Muslim population of Kerala (~88L Muslims, ~26% of Kerala state) are overwhelmingly Sunni Shafi'i — the only major Indian state where Shafi'i is the dominant madhhab (per Samastha Kerala / Malabar Muslims tradition rooted in Arab-trader settlement, popularized by the Maqdumis of Ponnani). The angle convention is Karachi 18°/18° (the South Asian regional convention used across India/Pakistan/Bangladesh per ITL/arabeyes cluster) but the Asr school is Shafi'i (shadow = object length), not the Hanafi default that the India country-dispatch returns. Mirrors the Maldives + Sri Lanka v1.7.1 explicit-Shafi pattern (same Karachi-cluster Shafi'i populations in coastal South Asia / Indian Ocean trading network).

altMethods.source: Some Bangsamoro diaspora apps (Muslim Pro defaults via NCMF Manila offices) use ISNA-aligned 15°/15° as a North America-influenced compromise; minority practice.

Cotabato.citation: Bangsamoro Darul-Ifta' (BDI-BARMM) — https://bdi.bangsamoro.gov.ph/ — Islamic advisory council of the Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM), promulgates fatwas including Ramadan moon-sighting determinations covering Cotabato City / Maguindanao / Lanao del Sur. Cross-referenced with National Commission on Muslim Filipinos (NCMF) — https://ncmf.gov.ph/ — and confirmed against al-hamdoulillah.com Cotabato default 'Muslim World League (MWL)' Fajr 18° / Isha 17° + Standard (Shafi'i) Asr.
Cotabato.reasoning: Cotabato City is the de facto seat of BDI-BARMM and a primary Maguindanaon-Muslim cultural centre. The Maguindanaon and Maranao Filipino Muslims follow Sunni Shafi'i fiqh (per Islam in the Philippines literature; majority madhhab of the Bangsamoro). Country-default for Philippines in fajr is already MWL (18°/17°) which matches the Aladhan world-default and which uses adhan.js Shafi'i Asr by default — so this override is included for explicit per-city institutional traceability (BDI-BARMM rather than generic MWL), same pattern as Bradford/Sarajevo. Pinpointed at Cotabato City coordinates.

altMethods.source: NCMF Manila-aligned ISNA 15°/15° used by some diaspora apps (Muslim Pro).

Marawi.citation: Bangsamoro Darul-Ifta' (BDI-BARMM) — https://bdi.bangsamoro.gov.ph/ — covers Lanao del Sur (Marawi City). Maranao Sunni Shafi'i tradition. Cross-referenced with NCMF and prayer-time third-party defaults at MWL 18°/17°.
Marawi.reasoning: Marawi City is the principal Maranao-Muslim city (Lanao del Sur, BARMM). Maranao tradition is Sunni Shafi'i. Same dispatch as Cotabato — MWL 18°/17° + Shafi'i Asr, with BDI-BARMM as the institutional source. Override exists for per-city institutional traceability.
